stem: Similar triangles ABC and DEF have perimeters 24 and 168, respectively, where side AB corresponds to side DE. If AB has length 10, what is the length of DE?
rationale: Choice A is correct. In similar triangles, corresponding side lengths share the same ratio as the perimeters. The ratio of perimeters is 168/24 = 7, so each side in triangle DEF is 7 times the corresponding side in triangle ABC. Therefore DE = 7 · AB = 7(10) = 70. Choice B is the perimeter ratio itself. Choice C is the sum of 7 and 8. Choice D is the reciprocal of the ratio.
